title: Favorite Version Of Fly Me To The Moon
display_title: Favorite Version Of Fly Me To The Moon
wikitext:
[]: Favorite Version Of Fly Me To The Moon             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sat May 05, 2012 12:11 am There are like, what, twenty to choose from from the series alone? My personal favorite is the one they play in Episode 16. The weird whispering sort of signifies that the show is going to take a darker shift in tone.

[]: #1 in France. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat May 05, 2012 12:28 am Here we go . If you meant series related, definitely this one , as it became facepalm-worthy within the first 15 seconds.

[]:             Quote Post by Eliaskar » Sat May 05, 2012 8:59 am Frank Sinatra version ofcourse.

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Sat May 05, 2012 10:01 am The instrumental one off of The Birthday of Rei Ayanami: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GAUXEk8cAVw

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sat May 05, 2012 11:38 am http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_NQ4NY0LgLM This remix.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat May 05, 2012 12:06 pm Aki Jungle mix . It's the only version of the song I can stand.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 05, 2012 12:21 pm I'm gonna troll you all and argue how "Beautiful world" is obviously superior to FMTTM in every single way.

[]: LOVE IT I SAY! BO             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat May 05, 2012 1:01 pm ^ Could care less, it would take you arguing Utada Hikaru's cover of FMTTM to be good to press my buttons. Bagheera wrote: Aki Jungle mix . It's the only version of the song I can stand.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat May 05, 2012 1:18 pm All I could say is, listening to Frank Sinatra's version is never the same. Yeah, the first version of the Evangelion's rendition spoiled me. (wait, let's be clear: the version heard in "Wall Street"- that's the only rendition that Sinatra did, right? My apologies for not being an expert in the field of Sinatra.)

[]: Re: LOVE IT I SAY! BO             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat May 05, 2012 1:58 pm Alaska Slim wrote: You tolerate this version because it's same-ish.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Mon May 07, 2012 3:56 pm The one they used first in the series. None of the others really do it for me.

[]:             Quote Post by MightyWings » Tue May 08, 2012 2:34 am Marvin Gaye's [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mxz8-xZisgE[/url] Frank Sinatra's is also boss.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Tue May 08, 2012 1:56 pm Frank Sinatra. Ain't gonna lie. Sorry, Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Tue May 08, 2012 5:29 pm Like others, I have to go with the Chairman on this one . It's a crime shame they never got a recording of Franky or his son singing the tune for the series or the movies. If they ever do decide to do it though, perhaps in a duet with Hikaru Utada or something, it would certainly do a lot to improve the enjoyment of the movie -- provided it isn't worse than Rebuild 2.22, somehow.

[]: Re: #1 in France. B/             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue May 08, 2012 6:42 pm Franky is untouchable. Alaska Slim wrote: If you meant series related, definitely this one , as it became facepalm-worthy within the first 15 seconds.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed May 09, 2012 3:06 am Frank is great but this one's not bad either.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat May 12, 2012 11:15 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Frank Sinatra. Ain't gonna lie. Sorry, Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by Kerath » Sun May 13, 2012 5:36 pm FRY ME TO THA MOON

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun May 13, 2012 10:27 pm I'd have to say this one Or this one ... Evan if it doesn't played in Eva...

[]:             Quote Post by bufula » Sun May 13, 2012 11:17 pm I've always preferred this version. [s]maybe it's because it reminds me of kaworu flying me to the moon??[/s]
summary:
[]: Favorite Version Of Fly Me To The Moon             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sat May 05, 2012 12:11 am There are like, what, twenty to choose from from the series alone? My personal favorite is the one they play in Episode 16. The weird whispering sort of signifies that the show is going to take a darker shift in tone.

[]: #1 in France. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim &raqu
